title: Qualitative Systematic Analysis of Carbohydrates (Glucose, Fructose, Lactose, Maltose, Sucrose and Starch)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"pf6ii","text":"Carbohydrates are polyhydroxyaldehyde or ketone derivatives of polyhydric alcohols with the general chemical formula Cn(H2O)n, and these are largely distributed in animals and plants. Carbohydrates provide energy through oxidation and supplies carbon for the synthesis of cell components; they serve as a form of stored chemical energy. Carbohydrates, along with lipids, proteins, nucleic acids, and other compounds are known as biomolecules because they are closely associated with living organisms (Table 40.1).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":121,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"s6ca3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
